mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid improvement in labor market conditions signals strengthening aggregate demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive equity returns over a one-week horizon as investors price in higher near-term cash flows.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.2 percentage points within one month
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or zero forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in industrial production and stable consumer price inflation indicates a healthy demand-supply equilibrium without overheating, which supports corporate margin expansion and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in more than 60% of rolling 3-year windows, or INDPRO/CPIAUCSL correlation breaks down without return impact.

mechanism_narrative: Diverging housing starts and GDP growth signals a decelerating demand cycle where real economic activity slows while construction activity lags, prompting equity de-rating and negative forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust business hiring and income expansion, which directly supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The monotonic relationship between PAYEMS growth and 5-day forward returns breaks down, yielding a negative or statistically insignificant correlation over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the federal funds rate above a critical threshold compresses equity valuations by raising the cost of capital and triggering risk-off positioning, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25 percentage points in a single month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 50% of instances following the threshold breach, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for VIX changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising revolving credit balances combined with falling unemployment indicate accelerating consumer leverage and labor market tightness, which fuels near-term consumption but eventually triggers earnings mean-reversion and negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to predict negative returns in more than 65% of out-of-sample periods, or RSAFS growth decouples from UNRATE without impacting equity valuations.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in the industrial production index following a contraction signals supply-side normalization and capacity utilization recovery, which lifts corporate profitability and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 1.0 percentage point from prior month
failure_condition: The signal yields negative or flat forward returns in more than 55% of triggered episodes, or the effect disappears when controlling for concurrent FEDFUNDS changes.
